Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>si</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-03-05</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
Creata. ✅

Da adesso puoi scrivere `/linkedin-post` e parto direttamente — ti chiedo solo argomento e dati, poi genero il post pronto per pubblicare.

Vuoi crearne altre tra le 3 rimaste (`email-cliente`, `quote-template`, `meeting-brief`)?

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>Write</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-03-05T15:08:50.033Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>/Users/royrigamonti/Chuck</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"file_path\":\"/Users/royrigamonti/Chuck/.claude/skills/email-cliente/SKILL.md\",\"content\":\"---\\nname: email-cliente\\ndescription: Bozza email a clienti o prospect Columbia Transport nel tono corretto di Roy. Usare quando l'utente chiede di scrivere un'email a un cliente, prospect o fornitore.\\n---\\n\\n# Skill: email-cliente\\n\\n## Regole Voce (da brand-voice.md)\\n\\n**Cliente/Prospect esterno:**\\n- Professionale ma diretto\\n- Numeri e specs prima, spiegazione dopo\\n- Breve — niente paragrafi lunghi\\n- Niente: \\\"I hope this finds you well\\\", \\\"Please don't hesitate\\\", filler\\n- Chiudi con: \\\"Best regards\\\" (EN) o niente (IT informale)\\n\\n**Team interno:**\\n- Casual, diretto, \\\"ciao amico mio\\\" se familiare\\n- Short forms: \\\"pls\\\", \\\"asap\\\", \\\"V\\\"\\n- Specs/numeri prima, istruzioni dopo\\n\\n## Procedura\\n\\n1. Chiedi: **\\\"A chi e' l'email?\\\"** (nome + ruolo/azienda se non specificato)\\n2. Chiedi: **\\\"Qual e' l'obiettivo?\\\"** (quotazione, follow-up, istruzione interna, altro)\\n3. Chiedi: **\\\"Hai dati specifici da includere?\\\"** (numeri, date, specs)\\n4. Genera la bozza\\n5. Mostra e chiedi approvazione — NON inviare mai autonomamente\\n\\n## Template per Tipo\\n\\n### Quotazione a Cliente\\n```\\n[Saluto breve]\\n[Specs spedizione: peso, volume, rotta, servizio]\\n[Tariffa netta]\\n[Validita' offerta]\\n[Eventuale nota su transit time o condizioni]\\nBest regards\\nRoy\\n```\\n\\n### Follow-up Post-Meeting\\n```\\n[Saluto]\\n[Recap sintetico di quanto discusso — bullet]\\n[Prossimi passi concordati]\\n[Richiesta conferma / call to action]\\nBest regards\\nRoy\\n```\\n\\n### Istruzione Interna a Team\\n```\\n[Nome], pls [azione specifica]\\n[Dettagli: cliente, numero pratica, scadenza]\\n[Eventuale recap per conferma]\\n```\\n\\n## Quality Checklist\\n- [ ] Nessun filler o frase vuota\\n- [ ] Numeri/specs presenti se rilevanti\\n- [ ] Tono corretto per destinatario (esterno vs interno)\\n- [ ] Breve e actionable\\n- [ ] Approvazione Roy prima di qualsiasi invio\\n\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"create\",\"filePath\":\"/Users/royrigamonti/Chuck/.claude/skills/email-cliente/SKILL.md\",\"content\":\"---\\nname: email-cliente\\ndescription: Bozza email a clienti o prospect Columbia Transport nel tono corretto di Roy. Usare quando l'utente chiede di scrivere un'email a un cliente, prospect o fornitore.\\n---\\n\\n# Skill: email-cliente\\n\\n## Regole Voce (da brand-voice.md)\\n\\n**Cliente/Prospect esterno:**\\n- Professionale ma diretto\\n- Numeri e specs prima, spiegazione dopo\\n- Breve — niente paragrafi lunghi\\n- Niente: \\\"I hope this finds you well\\\", \\\"Please don't hesitate\\\", filler\\n- Chiudi con: \\\"Best regards\\\" (EN) o niente (IT informale)\\n\\n**Team interno:**\\n- Casual, diretto, \\\"ciao amico mio\\\" se familiare\\n- Short forms: \\\"pls\\\", \\\"asap\\\", \\\"V\\\"\\n- Specs/numeri prima, istruzioni dopo\\n\\n## Procedura\\n\\n1. Chiedi: **\\\"A chi e' l'email?\\\"** (nome + ruolo/azienda se non specificato)\\n2. Chiedi: **\\\"Qual e' l'obiettivo?\\\"** (quotazione, follow-up, istruzione interna, altro)\\n3. Chiedi: **\\\"Hai dati specifici da includere?\\\"** (numeri, date, specs)\\n4. Genera la bozza\\n5. Mostra e chiedi approvazione — NON inviare mai autonomamente\\n\\n## Template per Tipo\\n\\n### Quotazione a Cliente\\n```\\n[Saluto breve]\\n[Specs spedizione: peso, volume, rotta, servizio]\\n[Tariffa netta]\\n[Validita' offerta]\\n[Eventuale nota su transit time o condizioni]\\nBest regards\\nRoy\\n```\\n\\n### Follow-up Post-Meeting\\n```\\n[Saluto]\\n[Recap sintetico di quanto discusso — bullet]\\n[Prossimi passi concordati]\\n[Richiesta conferma / call to action]\\nBest regards\\nRoy\\n```\\n\\n### Istruzione Interna a Team\\n```\\n[Nome], pls [azione specifica]\\n[Dettagli: cliente, numero pratica, scadenza]\\n[Eventuale recap per conferma]\\n```\\n\\n## Quality Checklist\\n- [ ] Nessun filler o frase vuota\\n- [ ] Numeri/specs presenti se rilevanti\\n- [ ] Tono corretto per destinatario (esterno vs interno)\\n- [ ] Breve e actionable\\n- [ ] Approvazione Roy prima di qualsiasi invio\\n\",\"structuredPatch\":[],\"originalFile\":null}"</outcome>

Chiedi: **\\\"A chi e' l'email?\\\"** (nome + ruolo/azienda se non specificato)\\n2. Chiedi: **\\\"Qual e' l'obiettivo?\\\"** (quotazione, follow-up, istruzione interna, altro)\\n3. Mostra e chiedi approvazione — NON inviare mai autonomamente\\n\\n## Template per Tipo\\n\\n### Quotazione a Cliente\\n```\\n[Saluto breve]\\n[Specs spedizione: peso, volume, rotta, servizio]\\n[Tariffa netta]\\n[Validita' offerta]\\n[Eventuale nota su transit time o condizioni]\\nBest regards\\nRoy\\n```\\n\\n### Follow-up Post-Meeting\\n```\\n[Saluto]\\n[Recap sintetico di quanto discusso — bullet]\\n[Prossimi passi concordati]\\n[Richiesta conferma / call to action]\\nBest regards\\nRoy\\n```\\n\\n### Istruzione Interna a Team\\n```\\n[Nome], pls [azione specifica]\\n[Dettagli: cliente, numero pratica, scadenza]\\n[Eventuale recap per conferma]\\n```\\n\\n## Quality Checklist\\n- [ ] Nessun filler o frase vuota\\n- [ ] Numeri/specs presenti se rilevanti\\n- [ ] Tono corretto per destinatario (esterno vs interno)\\n- [ ] Breve e actionable\\n- [ ] Approvazione Roy prima di qualsiasi invio\\n\",\"structuredPatch\":[],\"originalFile\":null}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

content.thinking:
This is a feature implementation - creating a new custom skill for email generation. The skill is called "email-cliente" and it's designed to draft emails to clients or prospects for Columbia Transport in Roy's voice/tone.

Key details:
- Name: email-cliente
- Purpose: Draft emails to clients, prospects, or suppliers for Columbia Transport
- Includes brand voice rules (from brand-voice.md)
- Different tones for external vs internal communication
- External: Professional but direct, numbers first, no filler
- Internal: Casual, direct, short forms
- Has a 5-step procedure for gathering info
- Includes templates for different email types: quotations, follow-ups, internal instructions
- Has a quality checklist
- Important: Never sends autonomously - always asks for approval first

This is a "feature" type observation.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Implemented email-cliente skill for client communication drafting</title>
  <subtitle>Custom skill generates client and team emails in Roy's voice for Columbia Transport business</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>email-cliente skill drafts emails to clients, prospects, and suppliers for Columbia Transport</fact>
    <fact>Skill enforces brand voice rules with different tones for external (professional, direct, specs-first) vs internal (casual, short forms) recipients</fact>
    <fact>Includes three email templates: quotations with shipping specs and rates, post-meeting follow-ups with action items, and internal team instructions</fact>
    <fact>Five-step procedure gathers recipient info, objective, and specific data before generating draft</fact>
    <fact>Quality checklist enforces no filler phrases, specs inclusion, correct tone, brevity, and mandatory Roy approval before sending</fact>
    <fact>External emails avoid phrases like "I hope this finds you well" and "Please don't hesitate"</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The email-cliente skill was created to generate email drafts for Columbia Transport business communications in Roy's voice. The skill implements strict brand voice guidelines that differentiate between external communications (professional but direct, numbers and specs first, no filler language, closing with "Best regards") and internal team communications (casual tone, short forms like "pls" and "asap", direct instructions). The skill provides three structured templates for common scenarios: client quotations with shipping specifications and pricing, post-meeting follow-ups with action items, and internal team instructions. A guided procedure asks for recipient details, email objective, and specific data before generating drafts. The quality checklist ensures all drafts are concise, actionable, and contain relevant specs while prohibiting autonomous sending without Roy's approval.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>why-it-exists</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>.claude/skills/email-cliente/SKILL.md</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

  <parameters>"{\"file_path\":\"/Users/royrigamonti/Chuck/.claude/skills/quote-template/SKILL.md\",\"content\":\"---\\nname: quote-template\\ndescription: Genera una quotazione rapida per cliente Columbia Transport partendo dal buying esistente. Usare quando l'utente fornisce costi di acquisto e vuole costruire una tariffa da proporre al cliente.\\n---\\n\\n# Skill: quote-template\\n\\n## Logica di Calcolo\\n\\n```\\nSelling = Buying + Margine\\nMargine % = (Selling - Buying) / Selling * 100\\nMOL spedizione = Selling - Buying (tutti i componenti)\\n```\\n\\nMargini target per servizio (da Marginalita-Servizi.md):\\n| Servizio | MOL % target |\\n|----------|-------------|\\n| Aereo AWB diretto | 25-35% |\\n| LCL consolidati | 18-28% |\\n| Aereo MAWB | 15-20% |\\n| FCL import/export | 8-18% |\\n\\n## Procedura\\n\\n1. Chiedi: **\\\"Servizio?\\\"** (FCL / LCL / Aereo AWB / Aereo MAWB)\\n2. Chiedi: **\\\"Rotta?\\\"** (POL -> POD)\\n3. Chiedi: **\\\"Buying costs?\\\"** (ocean/air freight + handling + altri)\\n4. Chiedi: **\\\"Cliente e' price-sensitive o priorita' e' il servizio?\\\"**\\n5. Calcola selling con margine target\\n6. Mostra breakdown completo per approvazione\\n7. Genera testo email quotazione (usa skill email-cliente)\\n\\n## Output Formato\\n\\n```\\nQUOTAZIONE — [Servizio] | [Rotta]\\n----------------------------------------\\nBuying:\\n  - Ocean/Air freight:  EUR ___\\n  - Handling origine:   EUR ___\\n  - Handling destino:   EUR ___\\n  - Altro:              EUR ___\\n  TOTALE BUYING:        EUR ___\\n\\nSelling proposto:\\n  - Ocean/Air freight:  EUR ___\\n  - Handling:           EUR ___\\n  - Altro:              EUR ___\\n  TOTALE SELLING:       EUR ___\\n\\nMOL:                    EUR ___ (___%)\\nValidita':              [data]\\nTransit time:           [giorni]\\n```\\n\\n## Quality Checklist\\n- [ ] MOL % rientra nel target per il servizio\\n- [ ] Tutti i costi di buying inclusi (no voci dimenticate)\\n- [ ] Validita' offerta specificata\\n- [ ] Approvazione Roy prima di inviare al cliente\\n\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"create\",\"filePath\":\"/Users/royrigamonti/Chuck/.claude/skills/quote-template/SKILL.md\",\"content\":\"---\\nname: quote-template\\ndescription: Genera una quotazione rapida per cliente Columbia Transport partendo dal buying esistente. Chiedi: **\\\"Cliente e' price-sensitive o priorita' e' il servizio?\\\"**\\n5. Calcola selling con margine target\\n6. Mostra breakdown completo per approvazione\\n7. Genera testo email quotazione (usa skill email-cliente)\\n\\n## Output Formato\\n\\n```\\nQUOTAZIONE — [Servizio] | [Rotta]\\n----------------------------------------\\nBuying:\\n  - Ocean/Air freight:  EUR ___\\n  - Handling origine:   EUR ___\\n  - Handling destino:   EUR ___\\n  - Altro:              EUR ___\\n  TOTALE BUYING:        EUR ___\\n\\nSelling proposto:\\n  - Ocean/Air freight:  EUR ___\\n  - Handling:           EUR ___\\n  - Altro:              EUR ___\\n  TOTALE SELLING:       EUR ___\\n\\nMOL:                    EUR ___ (___%)\\nValidita':              [data]\\nTransit time:           [giorni]\\n```\\n\\n## Quality Checklist\\n- [ ] MOL % rientra nel target per il servizio\\n- [ ] Tutti i costi di buying inclusi (no voci dimenticate)\\n- [ ] Validita' offerta specificata\\n- [ ] Approvazione Roy prima di inviare al cliente\\n\",\"structuredPatch\":[],\"originalFile\":null}"</outcome>

content.thinking:
Another skill implementation - this one is "quote-template" for generating pricing quotations for Columbia Transport clients.

Key details:
- Name: quote-template
- Purpose: Generate quick quotations for clients based on existing buying costs
- Includes pricing calculation logic (Selling = Buying + Margin)
- Has margin targets by service type:
  - Air AWB direct: 25-35%
  - LCL consolidated: 18-28%
  - Air MAWB: 15-20%
  - FCL import/export: 8-18%
- 7-step procedure that gathers service type, route, buying costs, client price sensitivity
- Calculates selling price with target margin
- Shows breakdown for approval
- Integrates with email-cliente skill for email generation
- Has a structured output format showing buying costs, selling price, MOL (margin), validity, and transit time
- Quality checklist ensures MOL% is within target range and all costs are included

This is a "feature" type observation.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Implemented quote-template skill for freight pricing calculations</title>
  <subtitle>Automated quotation generator calculates selling prices from buying costs with service-specific margin targets</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>quote-template skill generates customer quotations for Columbia Transport by calculating selling prices from buying costs plus target margins</fact>
    <fact>Margin targets vary by service type: Air AWB direct (25-35%), LCL consolidated (18-28%), Air MAWB (15-20%), FCL import/export (8-18%)</fact>
    <fact>Seven-step procedure gathers service type, route, buying costs, and client price sensitivity before calculating selling price</fact>
    <fact>Output format shows complete cost breakdown with buying costs, selling price, MOL (Margin on Logistics), validity date, and transit time</fact>
    <fact>Skill integrates with email-cliente skill to automatically generate quotation email text after approval</fact>
    <fact>Quality checklist validates MOL percentage against service targets and ensures no cost components are omitted</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The quote-template skill was implemented to automate pricing quotations for Columbia Transport freight services. The skill uses a structured calculation approach where Selling = Buying + Margin, with margin percentages varying by service type based on business targets defined in Marginalita-Servizi.md. The procedure guides users through gathering essential information (service type, route, buying costs including ocean/air freight and handling fees, and client price sensitivity) before calculating appropriate selling prices. The skill outputs a detailed breakdown showing all buying cost components, proposed selling prices, calculated MOL (both absolute and percentage), offer validity, and transit time. Integration with the email-cliente skill enables automatic generation of quotation emails. The quality checklist ensures margins stay within target ranges and all cost components are accounted for before sending to clients.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>why-it-exists</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>.claude/skills/quote-template/SKILL.md</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

  <working_directory>/Users/royrigamonti/Chuck</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"file_path\":\"/Users/royrigamonti/Chuck/.claude/skills/meeting-brief/SKILL.md\",\"content\":\"---\\nname: meeting-brief\\ndescription: Genera un brief pre-meeting su un contatto o azienda. Utile prima di incontri con clienti, prospect o partner. Usare quando l'utente dice \\\"ho un meeting con X\\\" o \\\"domani incontro Y\\\".\\n---\\n\\n# Skill: meeting-brief\\n\\n## Scopo\\nArrivare a ogni incontro preparato: chi e', cosa fa, cosa vuole,\\ncosa vuoi ottenere, come approcciarlo.\\n\\n## Procedura\\n\\n1. Chiedi: **\\\"Nome e azienda del contatto?\\\"**\\n2. Chiedi: **\\\"Hai gia' informazioni su di lui/lei?\\\"** (email passate, storico, settore)\\n3. Chiedi: **\\\"Obiettivo dell'incontro?\\\"** (primo contatto / sviluppo relazione / chiudere accordo)\\n4. Se il contatto e' in Gmail/Outlook: cerca email precedenti per contesto storico\\n5. Genera il brief — mostra per approvazione\\n\\n## Formato Output\\n\\n```\\nBRIEF — [Nome] | [Azienda] | [Data meeting]\\n============================================\\n\\nCHI E'\\n- Ruolo:\\n- Azienda:\\n- Settore:\\n- Sede:\\n\\nSTORICO CON COLUMBIA TRANSPORT\\n- Prima interazione / cliente da:\\n- Servizi utilizzati:\\n- Volume stimato:\\n- Note rilevanti:\\n\\nOBIETTIVO INCONTRO\\n- Cosa voglio ottenere:\\n- Cosa probabilmente vuole lui:\\n\\nPUNTI CHIAVE DA TOCCARE\\n-\\n-\\n-\\n\\nRISCHI / DA EVITARE\\n-\\n\\nFOLLOW-UP PREVISTO\\n- Azione post-meeting:\\n- Tempistica:\\n```\\n\\n## Uso Specifico — Montreal\\nPer i meeting di Montreal, aggiungere:\\n- Contesto mercato canadese (rotte rilevanti, volumi import)\\n- Lingua preferita (EN/FR)\\n- Potenziale sviluppo relazione (nuova vs esistente)\\n\\n## Quality Checklist\\n- [ ] Storico relazione incluso (se disponibile)\\n- [ ] Obiettivo incontro chiaro e specifico\\n- [ ] Punti chiave actionable, non generici\\n- [ ] Approvazione Roy prima di usarlo\\n\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"create\",\"filePath\":\"/Users/royrigamonti/Chuck/.claude/skills/meeting-brief/SKILL.md\",\"content\":\"---\\nname: meeting-brief\\ndescription: Genera un brief pre-meeting su un contatto o azienda. Utile prima di incontri con clienti, prospect o partner. Usare quando l'utente dice \\\"ho un meeting con X\\\" o \\\"domani incontro Y\\\".\\n---\\n\\n# Skill: meeting-brief\\n\\n## Scopo\\nArrivare a ogni incontro preparato: chi e', cosa fa, cosa vuole,\\ncosa vuoi ottenere, come approcciarlo.\\n\\n## Procedura\\n\\n1. Chiedi: **\\\"Nome e azienda del contatto?\\\"**\\n2. Chiedi: **\\\"Hai gia' informazioni su di lui/lei?\\\"** (email passate, storico, settore)\\n3. Chiedi: **\\\"Obiettivo dell'incontro?\\\"** (primo contatto / sviluppo relazione / chiudere accordo)\\n4. Se il contatto e' in Gmail/Outlook: cerca email precedenti per contesto storico\\n5. Genera il brief — mostra per approvazione\\n\\n## Formato Output\\n\\n```\\nBRIEF — [Nome] | [Azienda] | [Data meeting]\\n============================================\\n\\nCHI E'\\n- Ruolo:\\n- Azienda:\\n- Settore:\\n- Sede:\\n\\nSTORICO CON COLUMBIA TRANSPORT\\n- Prima interazione / cliente da:\\n- Servizi utilizzati:\\n- Volume stimato:\\n- Note rilevanti:\\n\\nOBIETTIVO INCONTRO\\n- Cosa voglio ottenere:\\n- Cosa probabilmente vuole lui:\\n\\nPUNTI CHIAVE DA TOCCARE\\n-\\n-\\n-\\n\\nRISCHI / DA EVITARE\\n-\\n\\nFOLLOW-UP PREVISTO\\n- Azione post-meeting:\\n- Tempistica:\\n```\\n\\n## Uso Specifico — Montreal\\nPer i meeting di Montreal, aggiungere:\\n- Contesto mercato canadese (rotte rilevanti, volumi import)\\n- Lingua preferita (EN/FR)\\n- Potenziale sviluppo relazione (nuova vs esistente)\\n\\n## Quality Checklist\\n- [ ] Storico relazione incluso (se disponibile)\\n- [ ] Obiettivo incontro chiaro e specifico\\n- [ ] Punti chiave actionable, non generici\\n- [ ] Approvazione Roy prima di usarlo\\n\",\"structuredPatch\":[],\"originalFile\":null}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

Genera il brief — mostra per approvazione\\n\\n## Formato Output\\n\\n```\\nBRIEF — [Nome] | [Azienda] | [Data meeting]\\n============================================\\n\\nCHI E'\\n- Ruolo:\\n- Azienda:\\n- Settore:\\n- Sede:\\n\\nSTORICO CON COLUMBIA TRANSPORT\\n- Prima interazione / cliente da:\\n- Servizi utilizzati:\\n- Volume stimato:\\n- Note rilevanti:\\n\\nOBIETTIVO INCONTRO\\n- Cosa voglio ottenere:\\n- Cosa probabilmente vuole lui:\\n\\nPUNTI CHIAVE DA TOCCARE\\n-\\n-\\n-\\n\\nRISCHI / DA EVITARE\\n-\\n\\nFOLLOW-UP PREVISTO\\n- Azione post-meeting:\\n- Tempistica:\\n```\\n\\n## Uso Specifico — Montreal\\nPer i meeting di Montreal, aggiungere:\\n- Contesto mercato canadese (rotte rilevanti, volumi import)\\n- Lingua preferita (EN/FR)\\n- Potenziale sviluppo relazione (nuova vs esistente)\\n\\n## Quality Checklist\\n- [ ] Storico relazione incluso (se disponibile)\\n- [ ] Obiettivo incontro chiaro e specifico\\n- [ ] Punti chiave actionable, non generici\\n- [ ] Approvazione Roy prima di usarlo\\n\",\"structuredPatch\":[],\"originalFile\":null}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

content.thinking:
Third skill implementation - "meeting-brief" for generating pre-meeting briefings on contacts or companies.

Key details:
- Name: meeting-brief
- Purpose: Generate pre-meeting briefs on contacts or companies for client/prospect/partner meetings
- Triggered when user says "ho un meeting con X" or "domani incontro Y"
- Goal: Arrive prepared knowing who they are, what they do, what they want, what you want, how to approach them
- 5-step procedure that gathers contact name/company, existing information, meeting objective
- Can search Gmail/Outlook for previous email history if contact exists there
- Structured output format includes:
  - WHO THEY ARE (role, company, sector, location)
  - HISTORY WITH COLUMBIA TRANSPORT (first interaction, services used, volume, notes)
  - MEETING OBJECTIVE (what Roy wants, what they likely want)
  - KEY POINTS TO COVER
  - RISKS / THINGS TO AVOID
  - EXPECTED FOLLOW-UP (action, timeline)
- Special section for Montreal meetings: Canadian market context, language preference (EN/FR), relationship potential
- Quality checklist ensures relationship history is included, objective is clear and specific, key points are actionable

This is a "feature" type observation.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Implemented meeting-brief skill for pre-meeting contact preparation</title>
  <subtitle>Generates structured briefings with contact history, meeting objectives, and actionable talking points before client meetings</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>meeting-brief skill generates pre-meeting preparation documents for encounters with clients, prospects, or partners</fact>
    <fact>Five-step procedure gathers contact name, company, existing information, and meeting objective before generating brief</fact>
    <fact>Skill integrates with Gmail/Outlook to search previous email history and extract relationship context</fact>
    <fact>Structured output includes contact profile, Columbia Transport relationship history, meeting objectives from both perspectives, key talking points, risks to avoid, and follow-up actions</fact>
    <fact>Special Montreal meeting mode adds Canadian market context, language preference (EN/FR), and relationship development potential</fact>
    <fact>Quality checklist ensures relationship history is complete, objectives are specific, and talking points are actionable before Roy's approval</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The meeting-brief skill was created to prepare Roy for business meetings by generating comprehensive briefing documents on contacts and companies. The skill follows a five-step procedure that gathers essential information about the contact (name, company, existing knowledge) and meeting goals (first contact, relationship development, or deal closure). Integration with Gmail/Outlook enables automatic extraction of previous email exchanges to build relationship history. The output format provides a complete picture including contact profile (role, company, sector, location), Columbia Transport relationship history (services used, volume estimates, relevant notes), dual-perspective objectives (what Roy wants to achieve and what the contact likely wants), actionable talking points, potential risks to avoid, and planned follow-up actions with timelines. A special Montreal meeting enhancement adds Canadian market context, language preferences, and relationship potential assessment for Roy's travel meetings. The quality checklist ensures briefs are thorough, specific, and actionable before use.</narrative>
